description: Mole ratios from balanced equations, mass-to-mass stoichiometric calculations, and introductory limiting reactant problems.

problems.problem: What is stoichiometry and what does it allow us to calculate?

step_by_step.explanation: Stoichiometry is the quantitative study of the relationships between reactants and products in a chemical reaction.
It allows us to predict the amounts of reactants needed or products formed in a chemical reaction.

problems.final_answer: Stoichiometry is the calculation of quantities in chemical reactions using balanced equations.

problems.problem: For the reaction: $2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$, what is the mole ratio of $\text{H}_2$ to $\text{O}_2$?
For the reaction: $4\text{NH}_3 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{NO} + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$, how many moles of $\text{O}_2$ are needed to react with $2.0$ mol of $\text{NH}_3$?
For the reaction: $\text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3$, how many moles of $\text{NH}_3$ are produced from $3.0$ mol of $\text{H}_2$?
For the reaction: $2\text{Mg} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{MgO}$, how many moles of $\text{Mg}$ are needed to produce $4.0$ mol of $\text{MgO}$?
How many grams of oxygen gas ($\text{O}_2$) are needed to completely react with $8.0$ g of hydrogen gas ($\text{H}_2$) according to: $2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{H}_2 = 2.016$ g/mol
moles $\text{H}_2 = 8.0 \text{ g} \div 2.016 \text{ g/mol} = 3.97$ mol

step_by_step.step: Use mole ratio from balanced equation
step_by_step.calculation: moles $\text{O}_2 = 3.97$ mol $\text{H}_2 \times \frac{1 \text{ mol O}_2}{2 \text{ mol H}_2} = 1.985$ mol $\text{O}_2$

Molar mass $\text{O}_2 = 32.00$ g/mol
mass $\text{O}_2 = 1.985 \text{ mol} \times 32.00 \text{ g/mol} = 63.5$ g

problems.problem: How many grams of water ($\text{H}_2\text{O}$) are produced when $10.0$ g of hydrogen gas ($\text{H}_2$) reacts with excess oxygen?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 18.016$ g/mol
mass $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 4.96 \text{ mol} \times 18.016 \text{ g/mol} = 89.4$ g

problems.problem: How many grams of sodium chloride ($\text{NaCl}$) are produced when $20.0$ g of sodium ($\text{Na}$) reacts with excess chlorine gas ($\text{Cl}_2$)?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{Na} = 22.99$ g/mol
moles $\text{Na} = 20.0 \text{ g} \div 22.99 \text{ g/mol} = 0.870$ mol

Molar mass $\text{NaCl} = 58.44$ g/mol
mass $\text{NaCl} = 0.870 \text{ mol} \times 58.44 \text{ g/mol} = 50.8$ g

problems.problem: For the reaction: $\text{CaCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{CaO} + \text{CO}_2$, how many grams of $\text{CaO}$ are produced from $50.0$ g of $\text{CaCO}_3$?
For the reaction: $2\text{Al} + 3\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{AlCl}_3$, how many grams of $\text{AlCl}_3$ are produced from $10.0$ g of $\text{Al}$?
How many molecules of water are produced when $4.0$ g of hydrogen reacts with excess oxygen?
For the reaction: $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$, how many grams of $\text{KClO}_3$ are needed to produce $5.0$ g of $\text{O}_2$?
How many grams of iron(III) oxide ($\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$) are produced when $5.00$ g of iron reacts with excess oxygen?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{Fe} = 55.85$ g/mol
moles $\text{Fe} = 5.00 \text{ g} \div 55.85 \text{ g/mol} = 0.0895$ mol

Molar mass $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 = (55.85 \times 2) + (16.00 \times 3) = 159.7$ g/mol
mass $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 = 0.04475 \text{ mol} \times 159.7 \text{ g/mol} = 7.15$ g

problems.problem: For the reaction: $2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + 7\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{CO}_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$, how many moles of $\text{CO}_2$ are produced from $3.0$ mol of $\text{C}_2\text{H}_6$?

step_by_step.calculation: moles $\text{CO}_2 = 3.0 \text{ mol C}_2\text{H}_6 \times \frac{4 \text{ mol CO}_2}{2 \text{ mol C}_2\text{H}_6} = 6.0$ mol $\text{CO}_2$

problems.problem: How many grams of oxygen gas are required to completely combust $10.0$ g of methane ($\text{CH}_4$)?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{CH}_4 = 16.04$ g/mol
moles $\text{CH}_4 = 10.0 \text{ g} \div 16.04 \text{ g/mol} = 0.623$ mol

Molar mass $\text{O}_2 = 32.00$ g/mol
mass $\text{O}_2 = 1.246 \text{ mol} \times 32.00 \text{ g/mol} = 39.9$ g

problems.problem: How many grams of water are produced when $10.0$ g of methane ($\text{CH}_4$) undergoes complete combustion?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 18.016$ g/mol
mass $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 1.246 \text{ mol} \times 18.016 \text{ g/mol} = 22.4$ g

problems.problem: How many atoms of aluminum are needed to produce $25.0$ g of aluminum oxide ($\text{Al}_2\text{O}_3$)?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 = 101.96$ g/mol
moles $\text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 = 25.0 \text{ g} \div 101.96 \text{ g/mol} = 0.245$ mol

problems.problem: What mass of silver chloride ($\text{AgCl}$) is produced when $5.00$ g of silver nitrate ($\text{AgNO}_3$) reacts with excess sodium chloride?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{AgNO}_3 = 169.88$ g/mol
moles $\text{AgNO}_3 = 5.00 \text{ g} \div 169.88 \text{ g/mol} = 0.0294$ mol

Molar mass $\text{AgCl} = 143.32$ g/mol
mass $\text{AgCl} = 0.0294 \text{ mol} \times 143.32 \text{ g/mol} = 4.21$ g

problems.problem: For the reaction: $2\text{Na} + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl}$, how many grams of sodium are needed to produce $50.0$ g of $\text{NaCl}$?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{NaCl} = 58.44$ g/mol
moles $\text{NaCl} = 50.0 \text{ g} \div 58.44 \text{ g/mol} = 0.856$ mol

Molar mass $\text{Na} = 22.99$ g/mol
mass $\text{Na} = 0.856 \text{ mol} \times 22.99 \text{ g/mol} = 19.7$ g

problems.problem: How many molecules of oxygen gas are consumed when $5.0$ g of propane ($\text{C}_3\text{H}_8$) undergoes complete combustion?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 = 44.09$ g/mol
moles $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 = 5.0 \text{ g} \div 44.09 \text{ g/mol} = 0.113$ mol

problems.problem: What mass of carbon dioxide is produced when $15.0$ g of butane ($\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10}$) undergoes complete combustion?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10} = 58.12$ g/mol
moles $\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10} = 15.0 \text{ g} \div 58.12 \text{ g/mol} = 0.258$ mol

Molar mass $\text{CO}_2 = 44.01$ g/mol
mass $\text{CO}_2 = 1.032 \text{ mol} \times 44.01 \text{ g/mol} = 45.4$ g

problems.problem: For the reaction: $2\text{Al} + 6\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{AlCl}_3 + 3\text{H}_2$, how many grams of $\text{HCl}$ are needed to react with $5.00$ g of $\text{Al}$?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{Al} = 26.98$ g/mol
moles $\text{Al} = 5.00 \text{ g} \div 26.98 \text{ g/mol} = 0.185$ mol

Molar mass $\text{HCl} = 36.46$ g/mol
mass $\text{HCl} = 0.555 \text{ mol} \times 36.46 \text{ g/mol} = 20.2$ g

problems.problem: How many formula units of $\text{NaCl}$ are produced when $5.0$ g of sodium reacts with excess chlorine?

step_by_step.calculation:
Molar mass $\text{Na} = 22.99$ g/mol
moles $\text{Na} = 5.0 \text{ g} \div 22.99 \text{ g/mol} = 0.217$ mol

problems.problem: Verify the law of conservation of mass for the reaction: $2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ using $4.0$ g of $\text{H}_2$ and $32.0$ g of $\text{O}_2$.

step_by_step.calculation:
moles $\text{H}_2 = 4.0 \text{ g} \div 2.016 \text{ g/mol} = 1.98$ mol
moles $\text{O}_2 = 32.0 \text{ g} \div 32.00 \text{ g/mol} = 1.00$ mol

step_by_step.explanation: From ratio $2:1$, $1.98$ mol $\text{H}_2$ requires $0.99$ mol $\text{O}_2$ (available), $\text{H}_2$ is limiting.

step_by_step.calculation:
moles $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 1.98$ mol ($1:1$ ratio)
mass $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 1.98 \text{ mol} \times 18.016 \text{ g/mol} = 35.7$ g

step_by_step.explanation: Some $\text{O}_2$ remains unreacted, so mass of products = mass of water + mass of excess $\text{O}_2$ = total reactant mass

problems.final_answer: Total mass of reactants = total mass of products, verifying conservation of mass.
